neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: Ruhr is embedded in the EU single market; EU industrial and climate policy directly shapes its economy

neighbors.name: Euro Area
neighbors.relevance: Germany is the eurozone's largest economy; Ruhr industrial output is a key eurozone GDP component

neighbors.name: Visegrád Group
neighbors.relevance: V4 supplies automotive components and labor to Ruhr-connected supply chains

neighbors.name: Nordic Council
neighbors.relevance: Norwegian gas and Swedish iron ore are critical Ruhr industrial inputs

neighbors.name: Baltic Sea Region
neighbors.relevance: Hamburg and Baltic ports handle Ruhr export logistics; energy imports arrive via Baltic pipelines

links.description: EU emissions trading and Green Deal policies directly impact Ruhr steel and chemical producers
Ruhr industrial exports are a pillar of Germany's trade surplus within the eurozone
Integrated automotive and machinery supply chain between Ruhr OEMs and V4 component factories
Ruhr industry depends on Norwegian natural gas and Swedish iron ore for steel production
Rhine-Rhone corridor connects Ruhr to Mediterranean ports for southern export routes
Duisburg inland port is the primary European terminus of China-Europe rail freight
